inline_review:
commit b88605d71f6391392185a018d9074febd67b3c1c
Author: Yuan Liu <yuan1.liu@intel.com>

mm/memory hotplug/unplug: Optimize zone contiguous check when changing pfn range

This commit introduces a new pages_with_online_memmap field to track
contiguous zones, allowing pfn_to_page() to safely bypass further checks
when spanned_pages equals pages_with_online_memmap.

> diff --git a/mm/internal.h b/mm/internal.h
> index 0eaca2f0eb6a..9a779827f016 100644
> --- a/mm/internal.h
> +++ b/mm/internal.h
> @@ -824,21 +824,17 @@ extern struct page *__pageblock_pfn_to_page(unsigned long start_pfn,
>  static inline struct page *pageblock_pfn_to_page(unsigned long start_pfn,
>  				unsigned long end_pfn, struct zone *zone)
>  {
> -	if (zone->contiguous)
> +	if (zone_is_contiguous(zone) && zone_spans_pfn(zone, start_pfn)) {
> +		VM_BUG_ON(end_pfn > zone_end_pfn(zone));
>  		return pfn_to_page(start_pfn);
> +	}

Does zone_spans_pfn() need READ_ONCE() annotations since it is now part
of the lockless fast path?

zone_spans_pfn() reads zone->zone_start_pfn twice (once directly, once
inside zone_end_pfn()) without READ_ONCE(). A concurrent writer modifying
zone_start_pfn could cause the compiler to emit multiple loads yielding
different values, leading to a mismatched calculated end boundary.

Additionally, resize_zone_range() (called during hot-add) updates
zone->spanned_pages without WRITE_ONCE(), which might allow concurrent
lockless reads in zone_is_contiguous() to observe torn writes.

[ ... ]
> diff --git a/mm/memory_hotplug.c b/mm/memory_hotplug.c
> index 86d3faf50453..2aa67458a3f3 100644
> --- a/mm/memory_hotplug.c
> +++ b/mm/memory_hotplug.c
> @@ -493,11 +493,11 @@ static void shrink_zone_span(struct zone *zone, unsigned long start_pfn,
>  		pfn = find_smallest_section_pfn(nid, zone, end_pfn,
>  						zone_end_pfn(zone));
>  		if (pfn) {
> -			zone->spanned_pages = zone_end_pfn(zone) - pfn;
> +			WRITE_ONCE(zone->spanned_pages, zone_end_pfn(zone) - pfn);
>  			zone->zone_start_pfn = pfn;

By removing the clear_zone_contiguous() safeguard during hot-remove
(in remove_pfn_range_from_zone()), could this intermediate state expose
poisoned memory to lockless readers?

Here, spanned_pages is updated before zone_start_pfn. During this
window, spanned_pages could equal pages_with_online_memmap, making
zone_is_contiguous() return true.

A concurrent lockless reader (like compaction) checking zone_spans_pfn()
might use the old zone_start_pfn, evaluate to true, and call
pfn_to_page() on pages just poisoned by remove_pfn_range_from_zone().

Similarly, during hot-add, could zone_start_pfn be decreased before
memmap_init_range() runs, exposing uninitialized struct pages?

[ ... ]
> diff --git a/mm/mm_init.c b/mm/mm_init.c
> index cec7bb758bdd..4aaa42533fff 100644
> --- a/mm/mm_init.c
> +++ b/mm/mm_init.c
> @@ -958,6 +958,7 @@ static void __init memmap_init_zone_range(struct zone *zone,
>  	unsigned long zone_start_pfn = zone->zone_start_pfn;
>  	unsigned long zone_end_pfn = zone_start_pfn + zone->spanned_pages;
>  	int nid = zone_to_nid(zone), zone_id = zone_idx(zone);
> +	unsigned long zone_hole_start, zone_hole_end;
>  
>  	start_pfn = clamp(start_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
>  	end_pfn = clamp(end_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
> @@ -969,8 +970,19 @@ static void __init memmap_init_zone_range(struct zone *zone,
>  			  zone_end_pfn, MEMINIT_EARLY, NULL, MIGRATE_MOVABLE,
>  			  false);
>  
> -	if (*hole_pfn < start_pfn)
> +	WRITE_ONCE(zone->pages_with_online_memmap,
> +		   READ_ONCE(zone->pages_with_online_memmap) +
> +		   (end_pfn - start_pfn));
> +
> +	if (*hole_pfn < start_pfn) {
>  		init_unavailable_range(*hole_pfn, start_pfn, zone_id, nid);
> +		zone_hole_start = clamp(*hole_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
> +		zone_hole_end = clamp(start_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
> +		if (zone_hole_start < zone_hole_end)
> +			WRITE_ONCE(zone->pages_with_online_memmap,
> +				   READ_ONCE(zone->pages_with_online_memmap) +
> +				   (zone_hole_end - zone_hole_start));
> +	}

Does unconditionally adding the hole size to pages_with_online_memmap
cause over-accounting in SPARSEMEM configurations?

init_unavailable_range() uses for_each_valid_pfn() and skips unmapped
sections that lack a mem_map. If the full hole size is added anyway,
pages_with_online_memmap could artificially match spanned_pages for
zones with missing sections.

Could this make zone_is_contiguous() incorrectly return true, bypassing
pfn_valid() checks and potentially dereferencing missing sections?

Also, if memory is later hot-added into one of these boot-time holes,
will adjust_present_page_count() add the newly onlined pages to
pages_with_online_memmap again?

Since spanned_pages would remain unchanged by resize_zone_range(),
this double-counting would cause pages_with_online_memmap to permanently
exceed spanned_pages, breaking the contiguous optimization for this zone.
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Boot-time memory holes in SPARSEMEM cause `pages_with_online_memmap` to over-account, leading to invalid struct page dereferences.","severity":"Critical","severity_explanation":"In `memmap_init_zone_range()`, the entire size of any memory hole (`zone_hole_end - zone_hole_start`) is unconditionally added to `pages_with_online_memmap`. However, `init_unavailable_range()` uses `for_each_valid_pfn()` and skips unmapped sections (which lack a `mem_map` in SPARSEMEM configurations). Because the full hole size is added anyway, `pages_with_online_memmap` will artificially match `spanned_pages` for zones with missing sections. `zone_is_contiguous()` will incorrectly return `true`, causing `pageblock_pfn_to_page()` to bypass `pfn_valid()` checks. Dereferencing `pfn_to_page()` for a missing section yields a garbage pointer and causes a kernel panic."},{"problem":"Hotplugging memory into a boot-time hole causes double-accounting, permanently breaking the contiguous optimization.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"During boot, `memmap_init_zone_range()` explicitly adds the size of all memory holes to `pages_with_online_memmap`. If memory is later hot-added into one of these holes, `resize_zone_range()` leaves the zone's `spanned_pages` unchanged. Then, when the memory is brought online, `adjust_present_page_count()` adds the newly onlined `nr_pages` to `pages_with_online_memmap`. Because those pages were already counted during boot, they are double-counted, causing `pages_with_online_memmap` to permanently exceed `spanned_pages`. This causes `zone_is_contiguous()` to always return false for that zone, silently defeating the performance optimization the patch intends to provide."},{"problem":"Non-atomic updates to `zone_start_pfn` and `spanned_pages` during memory hotplug/remove expose uninitialized or poisoned memory to lockless readers.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"The patch removes the explicit `clear_zone_contiguous()` safeguard. During a hot-remove at the start of a zone, `shrink_zone_span()` updates `zone->spanned_pages` before `zone->zone_start_pfn`. During this window, `spanned_pages` equals `pages_with_online_memmap`, so `zone_is_contiguous()` returns true. A concurrent lockless reader (e.g., compaction) checking `zone_spans_pfn()` will use the old `zone_start_pfn`, evaluating to true, and then call `pfn_to_page()` on pages that were just poisoned by `remove_pfn_range_from_zone()`. Conversely, during hot-add, `zone_start_pfn` is decreased before `memmap_init_range()` runs, exposing completely uninitialized struct pages to lockless readers."},{"problem":"Missing `READ_ONCE()` and `WRITE_ONCE()` annotations on `zone_start_pfn` and `spanned_pages` cause data races in the lockless fast path.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"`zone_spans_pfn()` is part of the lockless fast path in `pageblock_pfn_to_page()`. It reads `zone->zone_start_pfn` twice (once directly, once inside `zone_end_pfn()`) without `READ_ONCE()`. A concurrent writer modifying `zone_start_pfn` can cause the compiler to emit multiple loads yielding different values, leading to a completely bogus calculated end boundary. Furthermore, `resize_zone_range()` updates `zone->spanned_pages` without `WRITE_ONCE()` (unlike `shrink_zone_span()`), meaning concurrent lockless reads in `zone_is_contiguous()` can observe torn writes."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit b88605d71f6391392185a018d9074febd67b3c1c
Author: Yuan Liu <yuan1.liu@intel.com>

mm/memory hotplug/unplug: Optimize zone contiguous check when changing pfn range

This commit introduces a new pages_with_online_memmap field to track
contiguous zones, allowing pfn_to_page() to safely bypass further checks
when spanned_pages equals pages_with_online_memmap.

> +	if (zone_is_contiguous(zone) && zone_spans_pfn(zone, start_pfn)) {
> +		VM_BUG_ON(end_pfn > zone_end_pfn(zone));
>  		return pfn_to_page(start_pfn);
> +	}

>  			zone->zone_start_pfn = pfn;

> @@ -958,6 +958,7 @@ static void __init memmap_init_zone_range(struct zone *zone,
>  	unsigned long zone_start_pfn = zone->zone_start_pfn;
>  	unsigned long zone_end_pfn = zone_start_pfn + zone->spanned_pages;
>  	int nid = zone_to_nid(zone), zone_id = zone_idx(zone);
> +	unsigned long zone_hole_start, zone_hole_end;
>  
>  	start_pfn = clamp(start_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
>  	end_pfn = clamp(end_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);

> +

> +		zone_hole_start = clamp(*hole_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
> +		zone_hole_end = clamp(start_pfn, zone_start_pfn, zone_end_pfn);
> +		if (zone_hole_start < zone_hole_end)
> +			WRITE_ONCE(zone->pages_with_online_memmap,

> +	}

cause over-accounting in SPARSEMEM configurations?

init_unavailable_range() uses for_each_valid_pfn() and skips unmapped
sections that lack a mem_map. If the full hole size is added anyway,
pages_with_online_memmap could artificially match spanned_pages for
zones with missing sections.

Could this make zone_is_contiguous() incorrectly return true, bypassing
pfn_valid() checks and potentially dereferencing missing sections?

Also, if memory is later hot-added into one of these boot-time holes,
will adjust_present_page_count() add the newly onlined pages to
pages_with_online_memmap again?

Since spanned_pages would remain unchanged by resize_zone_range(),
this double-counting would cause pages_with_online_memmap to permanently
exceed spanned_pages, breaking the contiguous optimization for this zone.
